title: Childhood brain tumours and use of mobile phones: comparison of a case-control study with incidence data.
summary: Commentary on the CEFALO case-control study regarding mobile phone use and brain tumour risk in children, highlighting the inconsistency between case-control results and stable incidence trends in registries.
key_findings: Incidence trends for brain tumours in children remain stable despite increased mobile phone use.
Potential risk may be restricted to heavy users or very early life exposure.
